24.7% ([[Nielsen ratings]]){{cite news|first=Rick |last=Kissell |title=Oscars on ABC Draw Largest Audience in 10 Years |url=https://variety.com/2014/tv/ratings/early-ratings-oscars-look-to-be-up-slightly-from-last-year-1201124513/ |work=Variety |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |date=March 3, 2014 |access-date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140308205925/http://variety.com/2014/tv/ratings/early-ratings-oscars-look-to-be-up-slightly-from-last-year-1201124513/ |archive-date=March 8, 2014}} | last = 85th | next = 87th }} The '''86th Academy Awards''' ceremony, presented by the [[Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] (AMPAS), honored the best [[2013 in film|films of 2013]] and took place on March 2, 2014, at the [[Dolby Theatre]] in [[Hollywood, Los Angeles]], beginning at 5:30 p.m. [[Pacific Time Zone|PST]] / 8:30 p.m. [[Eastern Time Zone|EST]]. The ceremony was scheduled well after its usual late-February date to avoid conflicting with the [[2014 Winter Olympics]].{{cite news|title=OSCARS: 2014 Academy Awards Set For March 2 |url=https://deadline.com/2013/03/oscars-2014-date-march-2-460884/ |website=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |access-date=March 25, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140829041704/http://deadline.com/2013/03/oscars-2014-date-march-2-460884/ |archive-date=August 29, 2014}} During the ceremony, the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences presented [[Academy Award]]s (commonly referred to as Oscars) in 24 categories. The ceremony was televised in the United States by [[American Broadcasting Company|ABC]], and produced by [[Neil Meron]] and [[Craig Zadan]] and directed by [[Hamish Hamilton (director)|Hamish Hamilton]]. Actress [[Ellen DeGeneres]] hosted the show for the second time, having previously hosted the [[79th Academy Awards|79th ceremony]] held in 2007.{{cite news|url= http://oscar.go.com/news/oscar-news/ellen-degeneres-returns-to-host-the-oscars |title=Ellen DeGeneres Returns To Host The Oscars |work=[[Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences]] |publisher=AMPAS |date=August 2, 2013 |access-date=August 2, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140905050112/http://oscar.go.com/news/oscar-news/ellen-degeneres-returns-to-host-the-oscars |archive-date=September 5, 2014 }} In related events, the academy held its 5th annual [[Governors Awards]] ceremony at the Grand Ballroom of the [[Hollywood and Highland Center]] on November 16, 2013.{{cite magazine|last=Sperling |first=Nicole |title=Angelina Jolie honored at 2013 Governors Awards |url=http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/11/16/angelina-jolie-honored-at-2013-governors-awards/ |access-date=January 30, 2014 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |publisher=[[Time Inc.]] |date=November 16, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140214204732/http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/11/16/angelina-jolie-honored-at-2013-governors-awards/ |archive-date=February 14, 2014}} On February 15, 2014, in a ceremony at [[The Beverly Hills Hotel]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]], the [[Academy Award for Technical Achievement|Academy Awards for Technical Achievement]] were presented by hosts [[Kristen Bell]] and [[Michael B. Jordan]].{{cite news|last=Alexander |first=Bryan |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/life/movies/2014/01/30/kristen-bell-micheal-b-jordan-science-technology-academy/5056627/ |title=Bell, Jordan to ''[[Gravity (2013 film)|Gravity]]'' with seven awards, ''[[Dallas Buyers Club]]'' with three, ''[[Frozen (2013 film)|Frozen]]'' and ''[[The Great Gatsby (2013 film)|The Great Gatsby]]'' with two, and ''[[Blue Jasmine]]'', ''[[The Great Beauty]]'', ''[[Helium (film)|Helium]]'', ''[[Her (2013 film)|Her]]'', ''[[The Lady in Number 6: Music Saved My Life]]'', ''[[Mr Hublot]]'', and ''[[20 Feet from Stardom]]'' with one. The telecast garnered nearly 44 million viewers in the United States, making it the most-watched Academy Award ceremony since the [[72nd Academy Awards]] in 2000.{{cite news|last1=Maglio |first1=Tony |title=Oscars Propel ABC to Most-Watched Week in 4 Years |url=https://www.thewrap.com/oscars-abc-tv-ratings-most-viewers-4-years/ |access-date=July 21, 2014 |work=The Wrap |publisher=The Wrap News Inc. |date=March 4, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140729215131/http://www.thewrap.com/oscars-abc-tv-ratings-most-viewers-4-years/ |archive-date=July 29, 2014}} ==Winners and nominees== The nominees for the 86th Academy Awards were announced on January 16, 2014, at 5:38 a.m. PST (13:38 [[UTC]]), at the [[Samuel Goldwyn Theater]] in [[Beverly Hills, California]], by [[Cheryl Boone Isaacs]], president of the academy, and actor [[Chris Hemsworth]].{{cite news|last=Whitty |first=Stephen |title=2014 Oscars: Jersey-set 'American Hustle,' outer-space 'Gravity' lead nominations |url=http://www.nj.com/entertainment/index.ssf/2014/01/2014_oscars_american_hustle_gravity_lead_nominations.html |access-date=January 16, 2014 |work=The Star-Ledger |publisher=Advance Publications |date=January 16, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140119082758/http://www.nj.com/entertainment/index.ssf/2014/01/2014_oscars_american_hustle_gravity_lead_nominations.html |archive-date=January 19, 2014}} ''[[American Hustle]]'' and ''[[Gravity (2013 film)|Gravity]]'' tied for the most nominations with ten each.{{cite news|last1=King |first1=Susan |title=2014 Oscar nominations: 'American Hustle' shuffles David O. Russell to front of the pack again |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscar-nominations-2014-main,0,500124.story#axzz2qbUhXVIM |access-date=January 16, 2014 |work=Los Angeles Times |date=January 16, 2014 |last2=Lynch |first2=Rene |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140116191647/http://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscar-nominations-2014-main%2C0%2C500124.story |archive-date=January 16, 2014}} The winners were announced during the awards ceremony on March 2, 2014. ''American Hustle'' became [[David O. Russell]]'s second consecutive film to earn [[List of films with all four Academy Award acting nominations|nominations in all acting categories]] and the fifteenth film overall in Oscar history to Alfonso Cuarón became the first person of Mexican descent to win [[Academy Award for Best Director|Best Director]]. With [[Matthew McConaughey]] and [[Jared Leto]]'s respective wins in the [[Academy Award for Best Actor|Best Actor]] and [[Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor|Best Supporting Actor]] categories, ''[[Dallas Buyers Club]]'' was the fifth film to win both male acting awards.{{cite news|url=https://www.tvguide.com/News/2014-Oscar-Winners-1078426.aspx |author=Eng, Joyce |date=March 2, 2014 |title=12 Years a Slave, Gravity Top Oscars |work=[[TV Guide]] |access-date=March 5, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140306012840/http://www.tvguide.com/News/2014-Oscar-Winners-1078426.aspx |archive-date=March 6, 2014}} Additionally, 3 other films (''American Hustle'', ''[[12 Years a Slave (film)|12 Years a Slave]]'', and ''[[The Wolf of Wall Street (2013 film)|The Wolf of Wall Street]]'') also received nominations for both Best Actor and Best Supporting Actor. [[Cate Blanchett]] became the sixth actress to have won both female acting awards in her career. [[Lupita Nyong'o]] was the sixteenth Oscar acting winner to win for a debut film performance and the ninth [[Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress|Best Supporting Actress]] recipient to achieve this feat. [[Academy Award for Best Original Song|Best Original Song]] co-winner [[Robert Lopez]] became the youngest individual to win all four major American entertainment awatds ([[Academy Awards|Oscar]], [[Emmy]], [[Grammy]], and [[Tony Awards|Tony]]) and the [[EGOT|12th person overall]] to earn these accolades.{{cite news|last=Brockell |first=Gillian |title=Robert Lopez joins the exclusive EGOT club [[Ezra Koenig]] || Performers || "[[The Moon Song]]" from ''[[Her (2013 film)|Her]]'' |- | {{sortname||U2}} || Performers || "[[Ordinary Love (U2 song)|Ordinary Love]]" from ''[[Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom]]'' |- | {{sortname||Pink|Pink (singer)|Pink}} || Performer || "[[Over the Rainbow]]" as part of ''[[The Wizard of Oz]]'' 75th Anniversary tribute |- | {{sortname|Bette|Midler}} || Performer || "[[Wind Beneath My Wings]]" during the annual ''In Memoriam'' tribute |- | {{sortname|Idina|Menzel}} || Performer || "[[Let It Go]]" from ''[[Frozen (2013 film)|Frozen]]'' |} == Ceremony information == [[File:Ellen DeGeneres 2011.jpg|130px|thumbnail|right|[[Ellen DeGeneres]] hosted the 86th Academy Awards|alt=Headshot of a blond haired female in her early fifties wearing a blue jacket over blue and white shirts.]] Despite the mixed reception received from the [[85th Academy Awards|preceding year's ceremony]], the academy rehired [[Neil Meron]] and [[Craig Zadan]] as producers for the second consecutive year.{{cite magazine|last=Breznican |first=Anthony |title=Oscar Return: Craig Zadan and Neil Meron to produce show again |url=http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/04/16/oscar-sequel-craig-zadan-and-neil-meron-to-produce-show-again/ |access-date=April 1, 2014 |magazine=Entertainment Weekly |publisher=Time Inc. |date=April 16, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131109182718/http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/04/16/oscar-sequel-craig-zadan-and-neil-meron-to-produce-show-again/ |archive-date=November 9, 2013}} However, actor [[Seth MacFarlane]] announced that he would not host the Oscars for a second time. In a statement released through [[Twitter]], he wrote "Traumatized critics exhale: I'm unable to do the Oscars again. Tried to make it work schedule-wise, but I need sleep."{{cite news|last=Abramovitch |first=Seth |title=Seth MacFarlane Bows Out of Oscars |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/seth-macfarlane-bows-oscars-525605 |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |publisher=Prometheus Global Media |date=May 20, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140608235108/http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/seth-macfarlane-bows-oscars-525605 |archive-date=June 8, 2014}} Furthermore, actress and comedian [[Tina Fey]], who co-hosted the [[70th Golden Globe Awards]] more than a month earlier with fellow ''[[Saturday Night Live]]'' alumnus [[Amy Poehler]], told ''[[Huffington Post]]'' columnist Mike Ryan that she would reject any offer to host an Oscar telecast commenting, "I just feel like that gig is so hard. Especially for, like, a woman – the amount of months that would be spent trying on dresses alone – no way."{{cite news|last=Griffith |first=Janelle |title=Tina Fey says 'no way' to hosting the Oscars |url=http://www.nj.com/entertainment/index.ssf/2013/02/tina_fey_says_no_to_oscars.html |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=The Star-Ledger |publisher=Advance Publications |date=February 27, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140417120323/http://www.nj.com/entertainment/index.ssf/2013/02/tina_fey_says_no_to_oscars.html |archive-date=April 17, 2014}} Shortly after the election of AMPAS president [[Cheryl Boone Isaacs]] in August 2013, Meron and Zadan announced that comedian and talk show host [[Ellen DeGeneres]] would host the 2014 ceremony.{{cite news|last=Lee |first=Chris |title=Ellen DeGeneres back to host 2014 Oscars |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/la-xpm-2013-aug-02-la-et-mn-ellen-degeneres-back-as-oscars-host-for-2014-20130802-story.html |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=Los Angeles Times |date=August 2, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140410121357/http://articles.latimes.com/2013/aug/02/entertainment/la-et-mn-ellen-degeneres-back-as-oscars-host-for-2014-20130802 |archive-date=April 10, 2014}} They explained their decision to bring back DeGeneres as host saying, "As a longtime friend, we had always hoped to find a project for us to do together and nothing could be more exciting than teaming up to do the Oscars. There are few stars today who have Ellen's gift for comedy, with her great warmth and humanity. She is beloved everywhere and we expect that the audience at the [[Dolby Theatre]] and in homes around the globe will be as excited by this news as we are."{{cite news|last=Gennis |first=Sadie |title=Ellen DeGeneres to Host the Oscars |url=https://www.tvguide.com/news/ellen-degeneres-host-oscars-2014-1068788.aspx |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=[[TV Guide]] |date=August 2, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140417110547/http://www.tvguide.com/news/ellen-degeneres-host-oscars-2014-1068788.aspx |archive-date=April 17, 2014}} DeGeneres expressed that she was thrilled to be selected to emcee the gala again, commenting, "I am so excited to be hosting the Oscars for the second time. You know what they say – the third time's the charm."{{cite news|last=Carter |first=Bill |title=Ellen DeGeneres to Host Next Year's Oscars |url=https://www.nytimes.com/2013/08/03/business/media/ellen-degeneres-to-host-next-years-oscars.html |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=The New York Times |date=August 2, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141017234353/http://www.nytimes.com/2013/08/03/business/media/ellen-degeneres-to-host-next-years-oscars.html |archive-date=October 17, 2014}} As with last year's theme of music and the movies, Meron and Zadan centered the show around a theme. This year, they christened the show with a theme of saluting movie heroes commenting, "By celebrating the gamut of heroes who have enriched our movie-going experience, we hope to create an evening of fun and joy. And that includes the filmmakers and actors who take risks and stimulate us with provocative subjects and daring characters. They are all heroes in the cinematic landscape."{{cite news|title=Oscars to Celebrate Movie Heroes |url=http://www.oscars.org/news/oscarsr-celebrate-movie-heroes |access-date=April 2, 2014 |work=Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences |publisher=AMPAS |date=January 14, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141015105225/http://www.oscars.org/news/oscarsr-celebrate-movie-heroes |archive-date=October 15, 2014}} To coincide with the theme, AMPAS presented an exhibition in the lobby of its Beverly Hills headquarters titled "The Oscars Celebrate Movie Heroes". The exhibit featured posters, photographs, and artifacts from 70 different films featuring literary, comic book, and real life heroes.{{cite news|last=Gettell |first=Oliver |title=Oscars to celebrate movie heroes at this year's ceremony |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscars-movie-heroes-celebration-2014-ceremony-20140114,0,6833327.story#ixzz2z5BQp9cv |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=Los Angeles Times |date=January 14, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140416233845/http://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscars-movie-heroes-celebration-2014-ceremony-20140114%2C0%2C6833327.story |archive-date=April 16, 2014}} Furthermore, actor [[Andrew Garfield]], who portrayed the [[Spider-Man|titular character]] in ''[[The Amazing Spider-Man (2012 film)|The Amazing Spider-Man]]'', was scheduled to appear onstage with five-year-old cancer survivor [[Batkid|Miles "Batkid" Scott]] with Garfield christening Scott as an "official superhero". The segment was scrapped, however, due to time constraints.{{cite news|last=Gettell |first=Oliver |title=Oscars: 'Batkid' appearance with Andrew Garfield cut at last minute |url=https://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscars-batkid-appearance-with-andrew-garfield-cut-at-last-minute-20140306,0,1973863.story#axzz2v7nrtsG3 |access-date=March 6, 2014 |work=Los Angeles Times |date=March 6, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140307004242/http://www.latimes.com/entertainment/envelope/moviesnow/la-et-mn-oscars-batkid-appearance-with-andrew-garfield-cut-at-last-minute-20140306%2C0%2C1973863.story |archive-date=March 7, 2014}} Several other people were involved with the telecast and its promotion. [[Tony Award]]-winning art director [[Derek McLane]] designed a new set and stage design for the show.{{cite news|title=Oscar Production Team Announced |url=https://deadline.com/2014/01/academy-announces-oscar-production-team-665004/ |access-date=March 5, 2014 |website=Deadline Hollywood |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |date=January 15, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140217192229/http://www.deadline.com/2014/01/academy-announces-oscar-production-team/ |archive-date=February 17, 2014}} Filmmaker [[Paul Feig]] produced and directed a one-minute trailer promoting the event featuring DeGeneres and 250 dancers dancing and lip-synching to the song "The Walker" by rock band [[Fitz and the Tantrums]].{{cite magazine|last=Hertzfield |first=Laura |title=Ellen DeGeneres and a sea of tuxes strut to Fitz & The Tantrums in new Oscar promo -- VIDEO |url=http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/12/19/ellen-degeneres-paul-feig-oscar-promos/ |access-date=April 1, 2014 |magazine=[[Entertainment Weekly]] |publisher=Time Inc. |date=December 19, 2013 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140220105319/http://insidemovies.ew.com/2013/12/19/ellen-degeneres-paul-feig-oscar-promos/ |archive-date=February 20, 2014}} During the ceremony, actor [[Channing Tatum]] introduced a group called "Team Oscar". The team consisted of six young film students from colleges across the country selected by AMPAS whose role was to deliver Oscar statuettes to the presenters during the gala.{{cite news |last=Lewis |first=Hilary |date=February 6, 2014 |title=Oscar College Contest Winners Announced |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/oscar-college-contest-winners-announced-677714 |url-status=live |work=The Hollywood Reporter |publisher=Prometheus Global Media}} Television personality and former [[Miss USA]] titleholder [[Rachel Smith]] hosted "Inside the Oscars", a behind-the-scenes video blog on the Oscar ceremony website.{{cite news |last=Smith |first=Rachel |date=January 15, 2014 |title=Oscar.com Goes Behind The Scenes Of Nominations With First Installment Of Exclusive Inside The Oscars Video Series |url=http://www.oscars.org/news/oscarcom-goes-behind-scenes-nominations-first-installment-exclusive-inside-oscarsr-video-series |url-status=live 15, 2014 |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences |publisher=AMPAS}} In December 2013, AMPAS unveiled a new brand identity for the organization and the Academy Awards by local firm 180LA; the Academy Awards' new logo formalizes the prior year's rebranding of the ceremony as ''The Oscars'', using a wordmark with a stylized letter "A" resembling a [[Spotlight (theatre lighting)|spotlight]] shining on an Oscar statuette (a similar effect is used in the Academy's new logo, but instead using a filled triangle).{{Cite web |date=2013-12-18 |title=Oscars unveils brand new logo |url=https://www.creativebloq.com/logo-design/oscars-new-logo-121310195 |access-date=2024-07-30 |website=Creative Bloq |language=en}}{{Cite web |last=Montgomery |first=Angus ===Box office performance of nominated films=== At the time of the nominations announcement on January 16, 2014, the combined gross of the nine Best Picture nominees at the American and Canadian box offices was $645 million, with an average of $72 million per film.{{cite news|title=2013 Academy Award Nominations and Winner for Best Picture |url=https://boxofficemojo.com/oscar/chart/?view=&yr=2013&p=.htm |access-date=January 17, 2014 |work=[[Box Office Mojo]] |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140118031239/http://www.boxofficemojo.com/oscar/chart/?view=&yr=2013&p=.htm |archive-date=January 18, 2014}} When the nominations were revealed, ''Gravity'' was the highest-grossing film among the Best Picture nominees with $256 million in domestic box office receipts.{{cite news|title=2013 Domestic Grosses (as of January 15, 2014) |url=https://boxofficemojo.com/yearly/chart/?asof=2014-01-15&view=releasedate&view2=domestic&yr=2013&sort=gross&order=DESC&p=.htm |access-date=January 17, 2014 |work=Box Office Mojo |date=January 17, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140222183852/http://boxofficemojo.com/yearly/chart/?asof=2014-01-15&view=releasedate&view2=domestic&yr=2013&sort=gross&order=DESC&p=.htm |archive-date=February 22, 2014}} ''Captain Phillips'' was the second-highest-grossing film with $105.5 million; this was followed by ''American Hustle'' ($105.4 million), ''The Wolf of Wall Street'' ($80.7 million), ''12 Years a Slave'' ($39 million), ''Philomena'' ($22.3 million), ''Dallas Buyers Club'' ($16.8 million), ''Her'' ($9.9 million), and finally ''Nebraska'' ($8.5 million). Of the top 50 grossing movies of the year, 47 nominations went to 14 films on the list. Only ''Frozen'' (1st), ''Despicable Me 2'' (3rd), ''Gravity'' (7th), ''The Croods'' (14th), ''Captain Phillips'' (29th), ''American Hustle'' (30th), and ''The Wolf of Wall Street'' (42nd) were nominated for Best Picture, Best Animated Feature or any of the directing, acting or screenwriting awards. The other top 50 box office hits that earned nominations were ''Iron Man 3'' (2nd), ''The Hobbit: The Desolation of Smaug'' (8th), ''Star Trek Into Darkness'' (11th), ''The Great Gatsby'' (17th), ''Jackass Presents: Bad Grandpa'' (31st), ''The Lone Ranger'' (38th), and ''Saving Mr. Banks'' (48th). ===DeGeneres' Oscar selfie=== {{main|Oscars selfie}} Prior to the introduction of the [[Academy Award for Technical Achievement|Academy Awards for Technical Achievement]] montage, DeGeneres and several ceremony attendees such as [[Bradley Cooper]], [[Jared Leto]], [[Jennifer Lawrence]], [[Julia Roberts]], [[Kevin Spacey]], [[Meryl Streep]], [[Angelina Jolie]], [[Brad Pitt]], [[Channing Tatum]], [[Lupita Nyong'o]], and Peter Nyong'o (Lupita's brother) participated in a group [[selfie]]. The resulting tweet initially [[Fail whale|disabled the site]] and was eventually [[List of most-retweeted tweets|retweeted]] on [[Twitter]] over 3.4 million times.{{cite news|last=Guyun |first=Jessica |title=Ellen DeGeneres selfie on Twitter is Oscar gold for Samsung |url=https://www.latimes.com/business/technology/la-fi-tn-ellen-degeneres-selfie-twitter-oscars-samsung-20140303-story.html |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=Los Angeles Times |date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140410203238/http://articles.latimes.com/2014/mar/03/business/la-fi-tn-ellen-degeneres-selfie-twitter-oscars-samsung-20140303 |archive-date=April 10, 2014}} It surpassed the previous record retweet of [[Barack]] and [[Michelle Obama]]'s post-[[United States presidential election, 2012|election]] hug photo, which had been retweeted 778,000 times, in just 35 minutes.{{cite news|last=Kuperisnky |first=Amy |title=Trending: Ellen's Oscars retweet record, Adele Dazeem and Rachel Canning |url=http://www.nj.com/entertainment/index.ssf/2014/03/ellen_selfie_oscars_retweets_terry_shipman_adele_dazeem_sats_rachel_canning_teresa_giudice.html |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=The Star-Ledger [https://www.theguardian.com/film/2014/mar/03/oscars-adele-dazeem-john-travolta-idina-menzel "Adele Dazeem dazzled in her Oscars glory-yet doesn't actually exist"], [[The Guardian]]; retrieved 25 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140418081519/http://variety.com/2014/film/news/oscar-night-flubs-flood-media-1201124674/ |archive-date=April 18, 2014}} According to a source for [[E!]], 'Frozen' Star Idina Menzel for Oscar Flub |url=https://www.rollingstone.com/movies/news/john-travolta-apologizes-to-frozen-star-idina-menzel-for-oscar-flub-20140305 |access-date=March 6, 2014 Some media outlets were more critical of the show. ''[[Pittsburgh Post-Gazette]]'' television critic [[Rob Owen (journalist)|Rob Owen]] wrote, "Ms. DeGeneres brought predictable respectability to Sunday's 86th Academy Awards. Too bad this particular brand of predictable respectability was a bore." He also criticized the clip packages saluting movie heroes as "big a waste of time as Oscar montages almost always are."{{cite news|last=Owen |first=Rob |title=Tuned In: Oscars dull despite DeGeneres |url=http://www.post-gazette.com/ae/tv-radio/2014/03/03/Oscars-dull-despite-DeGeneres/stories/201403030111 |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=[[Pittsburgh Post-Gazette]] |publisher=[[Crain Communications]] |date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140303165252/http://www.post-gazette.com/ae/tv-radio/2014/03/03/Oscars-dull-despite-DeGeneres/stories/201403030111 |archive-date=March 3, 2014}} Columnist [[Alan Sepinwall]] of [[HitFix]] commented, "It was a long, disjointed ceremony, and what was fun and likely to endure came entirely from the winners and their speeches." He went on to say that many of DeGeneres's stunts fell flat and that ''[[The Wizard of Oz]]'' 75th anniversary tribute "felt much too random."{{cite news|last=Sepinwall |first=Alan |title=Review: Pizzas, celebrity selfies and pointless montages at the 2014 Academy Awards |url=http://www.hitfix.com/whats-alan-watching/review-pizzas-celebrity-selfies-and-pointless-montages-at-the-2014-academy-awards |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=[[HitFix]] |date=March 2, 2014 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140409081250/http://www.hitfix.com/whats-alan-watching/review-pizzas-celebrity-selfies-and-pointless-montages-at-the-2014-academy-awards |archive-date=April 9, 2014}} Tim Goodman of ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'' said, "It was a turgid affair, badly directed, poorly produced and featuring an endless string of either tired or wince-inducing moments by DeGeneres, who, by the last 30 or so minutes, seemed to have given up entirely." In addition, he noted that the show was overstuffed with montages and stunts that dragged down the pacing of the telecast.{{cite news|last=Goodman |first=Tim |title=Oscars TV Review: Ellen Flops in Long, Boring, Self-Involved Show |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/review/oscars-tv-review-ellen-flops-685409 |access-date=April 1, 2014 |work=The Hollywood Reporter |publisher=Prometheus Global Medua |date=March 2, 2014 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140323092131/http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/review/oscars-tv-review-ellen-flops-685409 |archive-date=March 23, 2014}} Other media outlets received the broadcast more positively. Television critic Matt Roush of ''[[TV Guide]]'' commented that DeGeneres "made the Oscars' inevitable dull patches felt less painful than usual." He also praised the cast and several musical numbers from the show.{{cite news|last=Roush |first=Matt |title=Review: A Slice of Oscar History |url=https://www.tvguide.com/News/Oscar-Review-History-Ellen-DeGeneres-Selfie-1078738.aspx |access-date=March 5, 2014 |work=[[TV Guide]] |publisher=[[CBS Corporation]] |date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140417111803/http://www.tvguide.com/News/Oscar-Review-History-Ellen-DeGeneres-Selfie-1078738.aspx |archive-date=April 17, 2014}} [[Frazier Moore]] of the [[Associated Press]] lauded DeGeneres's performance writing that, "She seemed to be committed to an unspoken theme for the evening: Humanize Hollywood's glitterati for the viewers. In return, the stars were on their best behavior." He concluded, "All in all, a sleek show was the Oscarcast. Few bombshells, fewer embarrassments, from fade-in to fade-out."{{cite news |last=Moore |first=Frazier |title=Oscarcast: A fine scene hosted by Ellen DeGeneres |url=http://www.fortwayne.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=/20140303/ENT/320144503/1027 |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=[[The News-Sentinel]] |publisher=[[Ogden Newspapers]] |date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140417084716/http://www.fortwayne.com/apps/pbcs.dll/article?AID=%2F20140303%2FENT%2F320144503%2F1027 |archive-date=April 17, 2014 }} Entertainment editor Marlow Stern of ''[[The Daily Beast]]'' raved, "DeGeneres followed in the footsteps of the most successful awards show hosts—[[Billy Crystal]], the duo of [[Tina Fey|Fey]] & [[Amy Poehler|Poehler]], etc.—who have taken advantage of the audience, engaging in gleeful interactions with the plethora of A-listers there (when they're not mocking them)."{{cite news|last=Stern |first=Marlow |title=Surprise! Ellen DeGeneres is the Best Oscars Host in a Decade |url=http://www.thedailybeast.com/articles/2014/03/03/ellen-degeneres-delivers-the-best-oscars-hosting-stint-in-a-decade.html |access-date=April 16, 2014 |work=[[The Daily Beast]] |publisher=[[IAC (company)|IAC]] |date=March 3, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140409020818/http://www.thedailybeast.com/articles/2014/03/03/ellen-degeneres-delivers-the-best-oscars-hosting-stint-in-a-decade.html |archive-date=April 9, |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140729030617/http://variety.com/2014/tv/news/most-watched-oscars-in-14-years-return-of-scandal-power-abc-to-weekly-victory-1201125249/ |archive-date=July 29, 2014}} It is the second highest-rated Oscars telecast on 21st-century U.S. television, trailing only behind the [[72nd Academy Awards|72nd ceremony]] held in 2000. In July 2014, the ceremony presentation received eight nominations for the [[66th Primetime Emmy Awards|66th Primetime Emmys]].{{cite news|title=Primetime Emmys: Full List Of Nominees |url=https://deadline.com/2014/07/emmy-nominations-2014-nominees-list-emmys-801678/ |access-date=July 10, 2014 |website=Deadline Hollywood |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |date=July 10, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140710201048/http://www.deadline.com/2014/07/emmy-nominations-2014-nominees-list-emmys/ |archive-date=July 10, 2014}} The following month, the ceremony won one of those nominations for Outstanding Art Direction for a Variety, Nonfiction, Reality, or Reality-Competition Program (Derek McLane, Joe Celli, and Gloria Lamb).{{cite news|last1=De Moraes |first1=Lisa |title=Creative Arts Emmy Awards: HBO Grabs Most Trophies; 'Saturday Night Live' Top Winner Among Shows |url=https://deadline.com/2014/08/creative-arts-emmy-awards-2014-winners-live-819681/ |access-date=August 17, 2014 |website=Deadline Hollywood |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |date=August 16, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140817094136/http://deadline.com/2014/08/creative-arts-emmy-awards-2014-winners-live-819681/ |archive-date=August 17, 2014}} ==''In Memoriam''== The annual ''In Memoriam'' tribute was presented by actress [[Glenn Close]].{{cite news|last=Harrison |first=Lily |title=Bette Midler Sings During Oscars In Memoriam Tribute Honoring James Gandolfini, Philip Seymour Hoffman and More |url=http://www.eonline.com/news/516415/bette-midler-sings-during-oscars-in-memoriam-tribute-honoring-james-gandolfini-philip-seymour-hoffman-and-more-late-stars |access-date=March 5, 2014 |work=E! |publisher=NBCUniversal |date=March 2, 2014 | url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140303204548/http://www.eonline.com/news/516415/bette-midler-sings-during-oscars-in-memoriam-tribute-honoring-james-gandolfini-philip-seymour-hoffman-and-more-late-stars |archive-date=March 3, 2014}} The montage featured an excerpt of the main title from ''[[Somewhere in Time (film)|Somewhere in Time]]'' by composer [[John Barry (composer)|John Barry]].{{cite news|last=Bobbin |first=Jay |title=Oscars 2014: Bette Midler lends a mighty 'In Memoriam' assist |url=http://blog.zap2it.com/frominsidethebox/2014/03/oscars-2014-bette-midler-lends-a-mighty-in-memoriam-assist.html |access-date=March 5, 2014 |work=[[Zap2it]] |publisher=[[Tribune Media]] |date=March 2, 2014 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140306030111/http://blog.zap2it.com/frominsidethebox/2014/03/oscars-2014-bette-midler-lends-a-mighty-in-memoriam-assist.html |archive-date=March 6, 2014}} At the conclusion of the tribute, singer [[Bette Midler]] performed her song "[[Wind Beneath My Wings]]" from the film ''[[Beaches (1988 film)|Beaches]]''.{{cite news|last=Yamoto |first=Jen |title=Oscars: In Memoriam Tribute Includes 'Midnight Rider's Sarah Jones |url=https://deadline.com/2014/03/oscars-midnight-rider-sarah-jones-in-memoriam-tribute-692470/ |access-date=March 5, 2014 |website=Deadline Hollywood |publisher=Penske Media Corporation |date=March 2, 2014 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140305150009/http://www.deadline.com/2014/03/oscars-midnight-rider-sarah-jones-in-memoriam-tribute/ |archive-date=March 5, 2014}} Before "In Memoriam" and while co-presenting Best Cinematography, Bill Murray paid an additional tribute to [[Harold Ramis]]. After co-presenter Amy Adams presented the nominees, Murray ad-libbed "Oh, we forgot one.
